lections show that our model can signiﬁcantly outperform
some well-known retrieval models as well as state-of-the-art
deep matching models.

concepts.finding:
We propose a novel deep relevance matching model for
ad-hoc retrieval by explicitly addressing the three key
factors of relevance matching.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:

and keyword based without complex grammatical structures
in ad-hoc retrieval, it is important to take into account

concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
4.1
Model Training
Since the ad-hoc retrieval task is fundamentally a ranking
problem, we employ a pairwise ranking loss such as hinge
loss to train our deep relevance matching model.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

In
this work, we introduce a model speciﬁcally designed for the
relevance matching problem.

entative benchmark datasets show that our model can sig-
niﬁcantly outperform traditional retrieval models as well as
state-of-the-art deep matching models.

concepts.finding:
We show how our major model designs, including match-
ing histogram mapping, a feed forward matching network,
and a term gating network, address the three key factors in
relevance matching for ad-hoc retrieval.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
Although various deep matching models have been pro-
posed under such a general matching problem formalization,
most of them have only been demonstrated to be eﬀective on
a set of NLP tasks such as paraphrase identiﬁcation and QA
[11, 26].
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
DEEP RELEVANCE MATCHING MODEL
Based on the above analysis, we propose a novel deep
matching model speciﬁcally designed for relevance match-
ing in ad-hoc retrieval by explicitly addressing the three
factors described in Section 3.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
Overall, our
model is similar to interaction-focused models rather than
representation-focused models since the latter would inevit-
ably lose the detailed matching signals which are critical for
relevance matching in ad-hoc retrieval.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.open_question:
How to address the limitation described: However, in this work, we argue that the matching prob-
lems in many NLP tasks and the ad-hoc retrieval task are

concepts.finding:
Most NLP tasks concern semantic
matching, i.e., identifying the semantic meaning and infer-
ring the semantic relations between two pieces of text, while
the ad-hoc retrieval task is mainly about relevance match-
ing, i.e., identifying whether a document is relevant to a
given query.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization
